title: Metadata, Corpus Inventory, and Rights
objective: Build a versioned, fail-closed corpus model that identifies every uploaded text as a scholarly work and a separate rights asset, produces a deterministic inventory, and exposes chronology readiness without making legal or scientific sufficiency claims.
scope: Versioned general asset metadata and Style Over Time chronology fields
Explicit file, asset, work, edition, source, genre, audience, adaptation, collection, and normalization relationships
Asset-level rights state machine with separate upload, analysis, export, and public redistribution permissions
Versioned CSV template, field dictionary, valid and invalid fixtures, and actionable row-and-field validation reports
Canonical corpus inventory and upload-order-invariant inventory hashing
Metadata-change invalidation contract for dependent runs
Exploratory readiness rule for fewer than three chronology points or six independent works
English-only guided corpus-documentation UI connected after the P003 secure-intake boundary
Accessible visual Corpus Review with a work timeline, composition bars, rights action matrix, metadata completeness matrix, and readiness summary
non_goals: Automatic copyright or jurisdictional legal determination
Internet corpus collection, scraping, or source-text redistribution
Job lifecycle, retention scheduler, production isolation, or deployment
R stylo execution or any scientific result
Final Pinocchio corpus analysis or a two-version collation workflow
Runtime AI, login, analytics, permanent project storage, or lemmata.app integration

acceptance.criterion: A versioned schema distinguishes file, asset, independent work, edition, and source identities; documents chronology, genre, audience, adaptation, collection, and normalization fields; and rejects missing required relationships.

Versioned CSV templates and a field dictionary are accompanied by valid and invalid fixtures; validation reports identify actionable row, field, and stable error codes for missing fields, duplicate work identifiers, unmatched files, conflicting dates, and unknown controlled values.

The rights state machine preserves verified-open, analysis-only, permission-required, unknown, and excluded states while independently recording upload, analysis, export, and public redistribution permissions; unknown or permission-required assets cannot qualify for raw public export.
Canonical inventory hashing is invariant to upload order and deterministic across clean reruns, while any semantic metadata or rights change alters the hash and invalidates dependent run identity.

Style Over Time readiness requires at least three chronology points and six independent works; smaller corpora are machine-readably and visibly forced to exploratory status without presenting the threshold as scientific sufficiency or confound removal.

The English-only corpus-documentation UI explains required fields and actionable corrections without weakening P003 intake checks, retaining rejected payloads, running scientific analysis, or making automatic legal determinations.

Corpus Review provides an accessible work timeline, composition bars, rights action matrix, metadata completeness matrix, and readiness summary with text equivalents, responsive behavior, and no placeholder or visual claim resembling a computed stylometric result.

changed_files: P004 execution brief, native PromptEvent link, and in-progress Ticket
Startup, handoff, roadmap, threat, contract, and project-memory pointers for the active P004 boundary
Human-approved metadata interaction defaults and accessible visual Corpus Review contract
Full-history CI checkout repair, regression test, failed-run diagnosis, and green branch/main verification
Human-approved Corpus workflow, purpose-aware field, teaching, and implementation-order contract
Versioned corpus domain, immutable P003 catalog projection, multi-layer rights dependencies, and generated schemas
Purpose-aware actionable validation, conservative chronology readiness, canonical hashing, invalidation binding, and deterministic fixtures
Retained failed attempts plus three-pass adversarial domain-model review

Candidate versioned 58-column metadata CSV, exact external field-dictionary schema, distinct rights-source preservation, evidence-gated public redistribution, P003-preserving parser/exporter, deterministic templates, row-aware diagnostics, and canonical semantic round-trip tests; recorded exact-commit verification remains pending

commands.result: The failed Linux verify job exposed 61 unresolved historical commits under the default one-commit shallow checkout; the container job passed.
The CI hotfix and post-merge main tree each passed 233 tests, 100% measured statement and branch coverage, and every local project gate.
The isolated hotfix branch passed GitHub verify, SBOM/audit, and Linux container jobs.
The main branch passed GitHub verify, SBOM/audit, and Linux container jobs after integration.
The host shell had no uv executable; this invocation was retained as a failure and replaced by the repository's canonical .venv and verify commands.
Intermediate full-gate attempts correctly stopped on Ruff formatting and then on the no-remote-endpoint source scan; neither partial run was treated as passing evidence.
The stable domain-model snapshot passed 318 tests, 100% of 1,510 measured statements and 392 measured branches, strict typing, metadata, record-integrity, repository, supply-chain, and R-lock gates.
The guessed convenience script did not exist; canonical record-integrity and schema pytest targets were used instead and passed 31 tests.
The pushed P004 domain foundation passed GitHub verify with SBOM/audit and the canonical Linux amd64 container build.
After independent review fixes, 167 focused CSV, corpus, rights, and schema tests passed.

The first final-gate attempt stopped on three Ruff formatting differences; a later attempt passed 385 tests but correctly stopped at the 100% coverage gate with 99.51%. Neither partial run was treated as passing evidence.

The final local candidate passed 390 tests, 100% of 2,097 measured statements and 542 measured branches, strict typing, schemas, metadata, provenance integrity, repository scanning, supply-chain tests, and the locked R/stylo environment.
